awadhi_text:
श्रीगुरु चरन सरोज रज निज मनु मुकुरु सुधारि।
बरनउँ रघुबर बिमल जसु जो दायकु फल चारि।।
hindi_meaning: श्री गुरु के चरण-कमलों की रज से अपने मन-दर्पण को साफ करके, मैं रघुवर (श्री राम) के उस पवित्र यश का वर्णन करता हूँ जो चारों फल (धर्म, अर्थ, काम, मोक्ष) देने वाला है।
english_meaning: Having purified the mirror of my mind with the dust of my guru's lotus feet, I sing the pure glory of Raghuvara (Ram) — the giver of all four fruits of life.
lesson: Tulsidas begins not with his own name or ego — but with guru-pranaam and humility. The mirror of the mind must be purified (through practice) before the Divine can be reflected in it.

awadhi_text:
बंदउँ गुरु पद पदुम परागा। सुरुचि सुबास सरस अनुरागा।।
अमिय मूरिमय चूरन चारू। समन सकल भव रुज परिवारू।।
hindi_meaning: मैं गुरु के चरण-पद्म के परागकण को नमस्कार करता हूँ, जो सुरुचि, सुगंध और अनुराग से भरा है। वह अमृत की जड़ का सुंदर चूर्ण है जो सभी सांसारिक रोगों के परिवार को नष्ट करता है।
english_meaning: I bow to the pollen of my Guru's lotus feet — full of good taste, sweet fragrance, and pure love. It is the beautiful powder of the root of immortality, destroyer of all the ills of worldly existence.
lesson: The guru's grace (guru-prasad) is the medicine that cures bhava-roga — the disease of worldly attachment. Without that grace, spiritual transformation is very difficult.

awadhi_text: सिय राम मय सब जग जानी। करहुँ प्रनाम जोरि जुग पानी।।
hindi_meaning: सारे जगत को सीता-राममय जानकर दोनों हाथ जोड़कर प्रणाम करता हूँ।
english_meaning: Knowing the entire world to be pervaded by Sita and Ram, I bow with folded hands to all.
lesson: The vision of seeing the divine in everything and everyone — this is the highest devotion. It transforms how you look at every person and every situation. This is the practical meaning of 'Ram-maya sab jag.'

awadhi_text: मंगल भवन अमंगल हारी। द्रवहु सो दशरथ अजिर बिहारी।।
hindi_meaning: जो मंगल का भवन हैं और अमंगल के हरने वाले हैं, वे दशरथ के आँगन में खेलने वाले (श्री राम) मुझ पर कृपालु हों।
english_meaning: He who is the abode of all auspiciousness and the destroyer of all inauspiciousness — may that one who played in Dasharatha's courtyard (Ram) shower grace upon me.
lesson: Ram is mangal-bhavan — the abode of auspiciousness. In every morning prayer and every invocation, we call on this quality: the divine that transforms inauspiciousness (disease, sin, failure) into auspiciousness.

awadhi_text:
राम नाम मनि दीप धरु जीह देहरी द्वार।
तुलसी भीतर बाहेर जो चाहसि उजियार।।
hindi_meaning: तुलसीदास कहते हैं — हे जीभ! राम नाम की मणि-रूप दीपक को अपनी देहली (द्वार) पर रख दो। तब भीतर और बाहर दोनों जगह प्रकाश हो जाएगा।
english_meaning: O Tulsidas! Place the jewel-lamp of Ram's name on the threshold of your tongue's door. Then you will have light both inside and outside.
lesson: Ram's name is the lamp that illuminates both the inner world (mind, consciousness) and the outer world (circumstances). The threshold is the tongue — keep Ram's name ready on the lips and in the heart.

awadhi_text:
राम नाम नरकेसरी कनककसिपु कलिकाल।
जापक जन प्रहलाद जिमि पालिहि दलि सुरसाल।।
hindi_meaning: राम नाम नृसिंह (नरकेसरी) है और कलियुग हिरण्यकश्यप है। जापक जन (नाम जपने वाला) प्रह्लाद की तरह है — जिसे राम-नाम उसी प्रकार पालेगा और रक्षा करेगा।
english_meaning: Ram's name is the Narasimha (man-lion); the Kali Yuga is Hiranyakashipu. The one who chants the name is like Prahlada — Ram's name will protect and nurture him, destroying the demons.
lesson: The name of Ram is your protector in the age of Kali. Like Prahlada, who was surrounded by demonic forces yet protected by his divine name — your sincere chanting is your armor.

awadhi_text:
सुनहु भरत भावी प्रबल बिलखि कहेउ मुनिनाथ।
हानि लाभु जीवनु मरनु जसु अपजसु बिधि हाथ।।
hindi_meaning: भरत, सुनो — भावी (भाग्य/विधाता का विधान) बलवान होता है। हानि-लाभ, जीवन-मृत्यु, यश-अपयश — ये सब विधाता के हाथ में हैं।
english_meaning: O Bharata, hear — the future (Vidhata's will) is powerful. Gain and loss, life and death, honor and dishonor — all these are in the Creator's hands.
lesson: Some things are not in our control. The wise person accepts this — not as defeat but as surrender. What IS in our control is our response, our sincerity, our effort. The rest is Vidhi (the Divine Will).

awadhi_text:
प्रथम भगति संतन्ह कर संगा। दूसरि रति मम कथा प्रसंगा।।
गुरु पद पंकज सेवा तीसरि भगति अमान।
चौथि भगति मम गुन गन करइ कपट तजि गान।।
hindi_meaning: पहली भक्ति — संतों का संग। दूसरी — मेरी कथाओं में अनुराग। तीसरी — मान रहित होकर गुरु के चरण-कमलों की सेवा। चौथी — कपट छोड़कर मेरे गुणों का गान।
english_meaning: The first form of bhakti: the company of saints (satsang). Second: love for my stories and discourses. Third: service of the Guru's lotus feet without pride. Fourth: singing my glories with sincerity, abandoning deceit.
lesson: Ram's own teaching on the nine forms of bhakti to Shabari. The first is satsang — the company of virtuous, sincere seekers. This is why the environment of practice matters so much.

awadhi_text:
मंत्र जाप मम दृढ़ बिस्वासा। पंचम भजन सो बेद प्रकासा।।
छठ दम सील बिरति बहु कर्मा। निरत निरंतर सज्जन धर्मा।।
सातवँ सम मोहि मय जग देखा। मोतें संत अधिक करि लेखा।।
आठवँ जथालाभ संतोषा। सपनेहुँ नहिं देखइ परदोषा।।
नवम सरल सब सन छलहीना। मम भरोस हियँ हरष न दीना।।
hindi_meaning: पाँचवाँ — दृढ़ विश्वास के साथ मंत्र-जप (वेद-प्रकाशित)। छठा — इन्द्रिय-दमन, सदाचार, अनेक कर्मों से विरक्ति और सदा सज्जन-धर्म में लगना। सातवाँ — जगत को मुझमय देखना और संतों को मुझसे भी अधिक मानना। आठवाँ — जो मिले उसमें संतोष और स्वप्न में भी दूसरों के दोष न देखना। नौवाँ — सबके साथ सरलता और छल-रहित व्यवहार, तथा हृदय में मेरा भरोसा — न हर्ष में न दीनता में।
english_meaning: Fifth: firm faith and mantra-japa as revealed by the Vedas. Sixth: control of senses, good conduct, detachment from many actions, constant devotion to righteous conduct. Seventh: seeing the world as pervaded by Me and considering saints even greater than Me. Eighth: contentment with whatever one gets, and never seeing faults in others even in dreams. Ninth: simplicity with all, freedom from deceit, and firm faith in Me — neither elated in joy nor low in sorrow.
lesson: Ram's complete teaching on navadha bhakti — the nine forms of devotion. Each form is a complete practice in itself. The ninth — sarlata (simplicity), freedom from deception, and equanimity in Ram's trust — is the crown of all.

awadhi_text: बिनु सतसंग बिबेक न होई। राम कृपा बिनु सुलभ न सोई।।
hindi_meaning: सत्संग के बिना विवेक नहीं होता। और सत्संग, राम की कृपा के बिना सुलभ नहीं।
english_meaning: Without the company of saints (satsang), discrimination (viveka) does not arise. And that satsang is not easily available without Ram's grace.
lesson: The chain: Ram's grace → satsang → viveka. If you feel drawn toward good company, good books, sincere seekers — that is Ram's grace operating. Don't dismiss it. Follow it.

awadhi_text: परहित सरिस धरम नहिं भाई। पर पीड़ा सम नहिं अधमाई।।
hindi_meaning: परोपकार के समान कोई धर्म नहीं है, और दूसरों को पीड़ा देने जैसी कोई अधमता नहीं।
english_meaning: There is no dharma equal to service of others (par-hita). There is no wickedness equal to causing pain to others.
lesson: Dharma simplified to its essence: help; don't hurt. The person who is honest in relationships — who does not objectify, who does not use others for gratification — is living the highest dharma.

awadhi_text:
जामवन्त के वचन सुहाए। सुनत हनुमान हृदय अति भाए।।
तब लगि मोहि परिखेहु तुम भाई। सहि दुख कंद मूल फल खाई।।
hindi_meaning: जामवंत के सुंदर वचन सुनकर हनुमान जी के हृदय को बहुत अच्छे लगे। (हनुमान ने कहा:) हे भाइयों! जब तक मैं लौटूँ, तब तक तुम मुझे प्रतीक्षा करो — दुःख सहकर कंद, मूल, फल खाते हुए।
english_meaning: Jambavan's inspiring words pleased Hanuman's heart greatly. (Hanuman said:) O brothers, wait for me until I return — enduring hardship, eating roots and fruits.
lesson: When Hanuman was told of his own power and divine potential, he rose to meet it. You also have more power than you think. Someone needs to remind you — let this practice be Jambavan for you.

awadhi_text:
जय हनुमान ज्ञान गुन सागर। जय कपीस तिहुँ लोक उजागर।।
राम दूत अतुलित बलधामा। अंजनि पुत्र पवनसुत नामा।।
hindi_meaning: हे हनुमान! आप ज्ञान और गुणों के सागर हैं, जय हो। हे वानरों के स्वामी! आप तीनों लोकों में प्रकाशित हैं, जय हो। आप राम के दूत हैं, अतुलित बल के धाम, अंजना के पुत्र, पवन के पुत्र।
english_meaning: Victory to Hanuman — ocean of wisdom and virtue! Victory to the lord of monkeys, who illumines all three worlds! You are Ram's messenger, the abode of incomparable strength, son of Anjana, son of the Wind.
lesson: Hanuman embodies the perfect combination: wisdom (jnana), virtue (guna), strength (bala), and complete devotion (seva to Ram). These are all built through disciplined practice — the same practice you are engaged in.

awadhi_text:
बुद्धिहीन तनु जानिके सुमिरौं पवनकुमार।
बल बुद्धि विद्या देहु मोहि हरहु कलेस विकार।।
hindi_meaning: अपने शरीर को बुद्धिहीन जानकर मैं हनुमान जी का स्मरण करता हूँ। हे पवनकुमार! मुझे बल, बुद्धि और विद्या दो और मेरे क्लेश तथा विकार हर लो।
english_meaning: Knowing this body to be devoid of wisdom, I remember Pavan Kumar (Hanuman). O son of the Wind! Grant me strength, intelligence, and wisdom, and remove my sorrow and impurities.
lesson: This prayer is honest self-assessment: I am not enough on my own. I need strength, intelligence, and the removal of vikara (impurities). This humility is the beginning of all real growth.

awadhi_text: प्रभु मुद्रिका मेलि मुख माहीं। जलधि लाँघि गए अचरज नाहीं।।
hindi_meaning: प्रभु (श्री राम) की मुद्रिका (अँगूठी) मुँह में रखकर (हनुमान) समुद्र पार कर गए — इसमें कोई आश्चर्य नहीं।
english_meaning: Taking the Lord's ring in his mouth, (Hanuman) crossed the ocean — there is no wonder in this.
context: Tulsidas says if you carry Ram's name (represented by the ring — mudrika) in your heart and on your lips, crossing even the greatest obstacles is no miracle — it is natural.
lesson: With Ram's name in your heart, you will cross your personal ocean — the ocean of lust, habit, addiction, doubt. Jaladhi langhi gaye — the ocean was crossed. With Ram's grace, yours will be too.

awadhi_text:
जड़ चेतन गुन दोषमय बिस्व कीन्ह करतार।
संत हंस गुन गहि पय परिहरि बारि बिकार।।
hindi_meaning: विधाता ने इस जगत को जड़-चेतन, गुण-दोष सब कुछ से भरा बनाया है। संत रूपी हंस इसमें से गुणों को दूध की तरह ग्रहण करते हैं और दोष-विकार को पानी की तरह छोड़ देते हैं।
english_meaning: The Creator made the world full of both inert and conscious beings, virtue and fault. The saints, like swans, extract the virtues like milk and leave behind the faults like water.
lesson: The swan (hansa) metaphor: in a mixture of milk and water, the swan can drink only the milk. Saints have developed the discernment to take goodness from any situation and leave the rest. This is viveka in action.

awadhi_text: नाम लेत भव सिंधु सुखाहीं। करहु बिचारु सुजन मन माहीं।।
hindi_meaning: राम नाम लेने से संसार-सागर सूख जाता है। हे सज्जनों! अपने मन में विचार करो।
english_meaning: By taking Ram's name, the ocean of worldly existence dries up. O virtuous ones, reflect on this in your minds.
lesson: Bhava-sindhu — the ocean of worldly existence with its craving and suffering — is dried up by Ram's name. This is the ancient promise. The sincere japa practitioner discovers this is not metaphor — it is experiential fact.

awadhi_text:
काम क्रोध मद लोभ रत गृहासक्त दुखरूप।
ते किमि जानहिं रघुपति महिमा मूढ़ मनुज अपकूप।।
hindi_meaning: काम, क्रोध, मद (अहंकार), और लोभ में रत, गृह में आसक्त, दुःख-स्वरूप — ऐसे मूर्ख मनुष्य रघुपति (श्री राम) की महिमा को कैसे जान सकते हैं?
english_meaning: Those immersed in lust, anger, intoxication, and greed — attached to home, the very form of suffering — how can such foolish people know the greatness of Raghupati (Ram)?
lesson: Kama (lust) is listed first because it is the root that feeds anger, ego, and greed. One who is ruled by kama cannot perceive the divine. This is not moral condemnation — it is practical observation: a clouded mirror cannot reflect light.

awadhi_text: राम भगत हित नर तनु धारी। सहि संकट किए साधु सुखारी।।
hindi_meaning: श्री राम अपने भक्तों के हित के लिए मानव शरीर धारण करते हैं और कष्ट सहकर साधुओं को सुखी करते हैं।
english_meaning: For the benefit of Ram's devotees, he assumes a human body — and enduring hardships, he brings happiness to the righteous.
lesson: Ram's avatar is out of love for devotees — not duty or compulsion. This is parama-prema. The divine does not abandon its own. Whatever hardship you endure in your sadhana, know that this love is present.

awadhi_text:
बंदउँ संत समान चित हित अनहित नहिं कोई।
अंजलि गहि आगे करउँ सुभ दृष्टि अवलोई।।
hindi_meaning: मैं उन संतों को नमस्कार करता हूँ जो समान चित्त वाले हैं और जिनके लिए न हित है न अनहित। अंजलि बाँधकर आगे करता हूँ, उनकी शुभ दृष्टि की प्रतीक्षा में।
english_meaning: I bow to those saints who are equal-minded, for whom there is neither friend nor enemy. I join my palms and extend them forward, awaiting their auspicious glance.
lesson: The saint is sama-chitta — equal-minded toward all. This equanimity is the fruit of long sadhana. Not indifference, but a universal compassion that does not pick favorites.

awadhi_text: एहि जग जामिनि जागहिं जोगी। परमारथी प्रपंच वियोगी।।
hindi_meaning: इस जगत की रात में केवल योगी ही जागते हैं — जो परमार्थ के खोजी हैं और जो प्रपंच (माया) से विरक्त हैं।
english_meaning: In the night of this world, only the yogis are awake — those who seek the supreme Truth and those who are detached from worldly appearances.
lesson: The metaphor of the world as 'night' — where most people are asleep in the darkness of maya. The yogi who wakes up at brahma muhurta and does sadhana while others sleep is literally living this verse.

awadhi_text: बिनु हरि कृपा मिलहिं नहिं संता। सत्संगति दुर्लभ संसारा।।
hindi_meaning: हरि की कृपा के बिना संत नहीं मिलते। संसार में सत्संग अत्यंत दुर्लभ है।
english_meaning: Without God's grace, saints are not encountered. Satsang is exceedingly rare in this world.
lesson: If you have found good company, good books, good teachers, a sincere community of practice — recognize this as divine grace. Durlabh — extremely rare. Honor it.

awadhi_text:
होइ न बिसय बिराग भव भोगन को पाइ।
काम क्रोध लोभ मोह मद इनहिं काहे न जाइ।।
hindi_meaning: संसार के भोगों को पाकर भोगों से वैराग्य नहीं होता। काम, क्रोध, लोभ, मोह, मद — ये भोगों से क्यों नहीं जाते?
english_meaning: By obtaining worldly pleasures, dispassion for pleasures does not arise. Why do kama, krodha, lobha, moha, and mada not depart through indulgence?
lesson: One of the most important practical truths: indulging desires does not end them — it strengthens them. Vairagya (dispassion) comes not from having but from withdrawing. Brahmacharya is the direct path to vairagya.

awadhi_text:
दया धरम को मूल है पाप मूल अभिमान।
तुलसी दया न छाँड़िए जब लग घट में प्रान।।
hindi_meaning: दया ही धर्म की जड़ है और अभिमान (अहंकार) पाप की जड़। तुलसीदास कहते हैं — जब तक शरीर में प्राण हैं, दया को मत छोड़ो।
english_meaning: Compassion is the root of dharma; pride (ego) is the root of sin. Tulsidas says: as long as life is in the body, do not abandon compassion.
lesson: The root of all dharma is daya — compassion. The root of all sin is abhimana — ego. Watch your ego carefully. When the ego says 'I am better than those who struggle,' — that is abhimana, the root of sin.

awadhi_text:
कामहि नारि पियारि जिमि लोभहि प्रिय जिमि दाम।
तिमि रघुनाथ निरंतर प्रिय लागहु मोहि राम।।
hindi_meaning: जैसे कामी को नारी प्रिय है और लोभी को धन — उसी प्रकार हे रघुनाथ (राम)! आप मुझे निरंतर प्रिय लगें।
english_meaning: As a lustful person loves a woman, as a greedy person loves money — may you, O Raghunath (Ram), be ceaselessly dear to me in the same way.
context: Tulsidas's audacious prayer: he wants the same intensity of love for Ram that a person has for their object of desire — unconditional, obsessive, consuming. He does not renounce the intensity; he redirects it.
lesson: The intensity of desire is not the problem — its direction is. Redirect the intensity of desire toward the Divine. This is the highest use of kama: sublimated into devotion (bhakti). Tulsidas himself shows the way.

awadhi_text: जे न मित्र दुख होहिं दुखारी। तिन्हहि बिलोकत पातक भारी।।
hindi_meaning: जो मित्र के दुःख में दुःखी नहीं होते, उनको देखना भी पाप है।
english_meaning: Those who do not grieve at a friend's grief — even looking at such people is a great sin.
lesson: True friendship is measured in pain, not pleasure. The one who is with you in difficulty — that is your true mitra. And you should be that person for others too.

awadhi_text: धीरज धरम मित्र अरु नारी। आपद काल परिखिअहिं चारी।।
hindi_meaning: धैर्य, धर्म, मित्र और स्त्री — ये चारों संकट के समय परखे जाते हैं।
english_meaning: Patience, righteousness, friend, and partner — these four are tested in times of adversity.
lesson: Your sadhana is testing your dhairya (patience) right now. Every day of practice, every urge resisted, is a test of patience. Dhairya is the first quality tested in adversity — and the most essential.

awadhi_text: सठ तोहि मोर सो रावन बोला। मरन काल मति होई खोला।।
hindi_meaning: रावण ने (विभीषण से) कहा — ओ मूर्ख! मृत्युकाल आने पर बुद्धि खो जाती है।
english_meaning: Ravana said (to Vibhishana): 'Fool! At the time of death, wisdom is lost.'
context: Ironically, Ravana speaks about himself — the person of great learning and power whose wisdom was destroyed by kama and pride. He who had studied all scriptures was ultimately destroyed by his own uncontrolled desire.
lesson: Ravana had great learning and power — but kama (desire for Sita) and mada (pride) destroyed him. Academic intelligence without self-mastery is dangerous. This is Ravana's cautionary tale for every scholar.

awadhi_text: साधन धाम मोक्षकर द्वारा। पाई न केहिं यह संगति सारा।।
hindi_meaning: यह सत्संग साधन का धाम और मोक्ष का द्वार है। इसे पाकर किसने उद्धार नहीं पाया?
english_meaning: This satsang is the abode of all spiritual practices and the gateway to liberation. Who, having obtained this company, has not been uplifted?
lesson: Satsang is the gateway to moksha. Your daily practice — your sadhana, your scripture study, your community of practice — is itself this gateway. The gate is open. Will you walk through it?

awadhi_text:
राम सो परम प्रेम जो तुम माँगेउ सुनि मुनि आयसु मोरे।
अस कहि राम मिले तब बोले काग सुखारे।।
hindi_meaning: श्री राम ने कहा: जो तुमने माँगा है वह परम प्रेम है। ऐसा कहते हुए राम मिले, तब काग (काकभुशुण्डि) सुखी हो गए।
english_meaning: Ram said: 'What you have asked for is supreme love (parama-prema).' Saying this, when Ram appeared (blessed), the crow (Kakabhushundi) was filled with joy.
lesson: Parama-prema — supreme love for God — is not below any worldly achievement. It is above all. When Ram Himself says 'what you asked for is the highest' — that is the value He places on sincere devotion.

awadhi_text:
तुलसी इस संसार में भाँति भाँति के लोग।
सबसे हिलिमिल चलिये नदी नाव संजोग।।
hindi_meaning: तुलसीदास कहते हैं — इस संसार में अनेक प्रकार के लोग हैं। सबसे मिल-जुलकर चलो जैसे नदी और नाव का संयोग।
english_meaning: Tulsidas says: in this world there are people of all kinds. Move with all harmoniously — like the joining of river and boat.
lesson: Social wisdom: don't isolate yourself in self-righteousness because of your practice. The boat (you) and the river (the world) work together — the boat uses the river, doesn't fight it. Navigate, don't judge.

awadhi_text: मुद मंगलमय संत समाजू। जो जग जंगम तीरथराजू।।
hindi_meaning: संत-समाज आनंद और मंगल से भरा है — वह जगत में चलता-फिरता तीर्थराज (तीर्थों का राजा) है।
english_meaning: The assembly of saints is full of joy and auspiciousness — it is the moving king of all pilgrimages in this world.
lesson: Satsang — the gathering of sincere seekers — is described as a moving pilgrimage. You don't need to go to Kashi or Prayag. Be among sincere, earnest people and you are already at the holiest tirtha.

awadhi_text: सकल पदारथ एहि जग माहीं। करम हीन नर पावत नाहीं।।
hindi_meaning: इस जगत में सभी पदार्थ (वस्तुएँ) विद्यमान हैं, परंतु कर्महीन (परिश्रमहीन) व्यक्ति उन्हें नहीं पाता।
english_meaning: All things exist in this world — but the person who does not act (the inactive one) does not obtain them.
lesson: This world has everything you need — but karma (right action, sincere effort) is the key. The person who sits passively waiting for transformation to happen without doing the work will not find it. Karam karo.

awadhi_text: जासु लोक पावन जग जाना। तासु स्वास स्वास राम राम बखाना।।
hindi_meaning: जिनके द्वारा जगत को पावन किया जाता है और जगत जिन्हें जानता है — उनकी हर श्वास में राम-राम ही है।
english_meaning: He whose every breath chants 'Ram Ram' — he purifies the world just by his presence.
lesson: The highest aspiration: to be so saturated with Ram's name that it flows with every breath. Begin with japa — conscious, counted repetition. Over years, it becomes ajapa — unconscious, spontaneous, with every breath.

awadhi_text: को कहि सकइ प्रयाग प्रभाउ। कलुष पुंज कुंजर मृगराउ।।
hindi_meaning: प्रयाग का प्रभाव कौन कह सकता है? यह पापों के समूह रूपी हाथी के लिए सिंह के समान है।
english_meaning: Who can describe the glory of Prayag? It is like a lion to the elephant of accumulated sins.
lesson: The power of sacred places, sacred times, sacred practices to dissolve accumulated karma (pap-punja — pile of sins). Your daily sadhana has this lion quality — it dissolves accumulated karmic debts with consistency.

awadhi_text: ग्यान अगिनि सब कर्म जरावा। जो जब राम सेवा चित लावा।।
hindi_meaning: जब कोई चित्त लगाकर राम की सेवा करता है, तो ज्ञान-अग्नि से उसके सभी कर्म जल जाते हैं।
english_meaning: The fire of knowledge burns all karmas — when one devotes the mind to serving Ram.
lesson: Jnana-agni — the fire of knowledge and devotion combined — burns karma. Your sincere daily practice IS jnana-agni. It is doing the purification work even when you cannot feel it.

awadhi_text: राम कथा सुंदर कर तारी। संसय बिहग उड़ावनहारी।।
hindi_meaning: राम की कथा सुंदर तालियाँ बजाने वाली (आनंददायी) है। यह संशय रूपी पक्षियों को उड़ाने वाली है।
english_meaning: Ram's story is beautiful — it is the clapping that drives away the birds of doubt. (Just as a clap scatters birds from a field, Ram Katha scatters the birds of doubt from the mind.)
lesson: When doubt comes — 'this practice is not working,' 'who am I to try,' 'this is impossible' — immerse yourself in Ram Katha (Ramcharitmanas). The doubts scatter like birds from a field.

awadhi_text: सोई जानइ जेहि देहु जनाई। जानत तुम्हहि तुम्हइ होइ जाई।।
hindi_meaning: वही जानता है जिसे आप (राम) जनाते हैं। और जो आपको जान लेता है, वह स्वयं आप ही हो जाता है।
english_meaning: Only the one to whom You reveal Yourself can know You. And the one who knows You — becomes You.
lesson: Self-knowledge is ultimately a matter of grace. The intellect can prepare the ground, but the final revelation is grace. Your practice prepares the ground. The seed is grace. Water both.
